diagrams.title: 2D Scalar Field Visualization
diagrams.description: A 2D plane showing a scalar field using a color gradient from cool (blue, low values) to warm (red, high values) with overlaid circular contour lines. A label 'Scalar Field f(x,y)' indicates this represents temperature, pressure, or altitude across a surface.
diagrams.purpose: Introduces the concept of a scalar field as a quantity that has a single value at each point in space, commonly used in computing to represent images, height maps, or density distributions

diagrams.title: 2D Vector Field Visualization
diagrams.description: A 2D grid of arrows at evenly spaced points. Each arrow points in a direction tangent to concentric circles (clockwise or counterclockwise flow), with arrow length varying to show magnitude. A label 'Vector Field F(x,y)' indicates direction and magnitude at each point.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how a vector field assigns a direction and magnitude to each point, essential for understanding forces, velocity fields, and flow in computing applications like fluid dynamics or particle systems

diagrams.title: Scalar Field with Gradient Vectors
diagrams.description: A side-by-side or overlaid view showing a scalar field (color gradient representing a hill or peak) with short arrows emanating from multiple points, all pointing toward areas of higher value (uphill). The arrows are longer where the gradient is steeper.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates that the gradient of a scalar field is a vector field pointing in the direction of steepest ascent, fundamental for optimization algorithms, machine learning (gradient descent), and understanding how to navigate scalar landscapes
